seoTitle: Derby vs Derwent Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Derby and Derwent Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Derby vs Derwent Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Derby and Derwent Park.
heading: Derby vs Derwent Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Derby, Tasmania and Derwent Park, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Derwent Park has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Derby (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Derby or Derwent Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Derby has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Derwent Park scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Derby scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Derwent Park scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Derby or Derwent Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Derwent Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
